title: If and Else
description: Teach your app how to make decisions.

slides.title: Making Choices 🤔
slides.content:
## Smart Applications

Up until now, our code runs straight from top to bottom, executing every single line.

But a real app needs to make decisions. *"If the user is logged in, show the dashboard. Else, show the login screen."*

slides.title: The 'if' Keyword 🚦
slides.content:
We use the `if` keyword followed by a condition in parentheses `()`. 

The code block inside the `{ }` will **only** run if the condition is `true`.

quizData.question: What is the requirement for the code inside an `if` block to execute?

options.text: The condition must evaluate to true.

quizData.explanation: The condition in the parentheses must be true for the code block to run.

slides.title: The 'else' Keyword 🛑
slides.content:
What if they fail? We can provide a fallback block using `else`.

If the first condition is false, the program will skip the `if` block and run the `else` block instead.

quizData.question: Can both the `if` block and the `else` block run during the same execution?

options.text: Yes, if the condition is exactly equal.
No, it's either one or the other.
No, because the compiler will crash.

quizData.explanation: If/Else represents a fork in the road. Only one path can be taken.
